US.name: United States of America
US.vat: None (State-level Sales Tax applies, generally 0% to 10.25%)
US.income_tax: Progressive federal brackets ranging from 10% to 37%, plus varying state and local income taxes.
US.self_employed_tax: Self-Employment Tax (SECA) of 15.3% (12.4% for Social Security and 2.9% for Medicare) plus standard federal and state income tax.
US.notes: Taxation varies significantly by state; some states like Florida, Texas, and Washington have no state-level personal income tax.

DO.name: Dominican Republic
DO.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 0% to 25% for individuals.
DO.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals must pay income tax based on the same progressive scale as employees and are generally required to make monthly advance payments (pagos a cuenta) of 1.5% on gross income.
DO.notes: Certain goods and services are exempt from ITBIS; the tax system is territorial for foreign-source income unless specific criteria are met.

MX.name: Mexico
MX.income_tax: Progressive rates ranging from 1.92% to 35% for individuals
MX.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals (RESICO regime) pay progressive rates from 1% to 2.5% on gross income for those earning up to 3.5 million MXN annually; others follow standard personal income tax brackets
MX.notes: Mexico utilizes the RESICO (Simplified Trust Regime) for small businesses and freelancers which significantly lowers the tax burden compared to standard personal income tax brackets.

CA.name: Canada
CA.vat: Varies by province: GST (5%) plus PST/HST (0-15%)
CA.income_tax: Progressive federal rates from 15% to 33%, plus provincial taxes
CA.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals pay personal income tax rates plus full CPP contributions (employer and employee portions)
CA.notes: Tax rates vary significantly by province; self-employed individuals must register for GST/HST if annual revenue exceeds 30,000 CAD

GB.name: United Kingdom
GB.self_employed_tax: Class 2 and Class 4 National Insurance contributions apply on profits above specific thresholds; Income Tax is levied at standard progressive rates
GB.notes: Income tax rates are different for Scottish taxpayers. Fiscal year runs from April 6 to April 5.

DE.name: Germany
DE.vat: 19% standard rate, 7% reduced rate for specific goods
DE.income_tax: Progressive tax rates from 14% to 42%, with a 45% top rate for high incomes over approximately 277,826 EUR
DE.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals pay standard income tax plus mandatory contributions for health and pension insurance; tax-free allowance of 11,604 EUR applies for 2024
DE.notes: Solidarity surcharge (Solidaritätszuschlag) of 5.5% applies only to high income earners since 2021

FR.name: France
FR.vat: Standard rate is 20%; reduced rates of 10%, 5.5%, and 2.1% apply to specific goods and services.
FR.income_tax: Progressive personal income tax scale ranging from 0% to 45% based on annual taxable income brackets.
FR.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals (micro-entrepreneurs) typically pay social security contributions based on a percentage of turnover (approx. 21.1% to 23.1% depending on the activity) plus a flat-rate income tax if eligible for 'versement libératoire'.
FR.notes: Tax rules vary significantly based on the chosen business structure (e.g., Micro-entreprise, EI, EURL, SASU) and geographical territory (Mainland France vs. Overseas Departments).

ES.name: Spain
ES.vat: Standard rate of 21% (reduced rates of 10% and 4% apply to specific goods and services).
ES.income_tax: Progressive tax scale for personal income ranging from 19% to 47% depending on annual income brackets.
ES.self_employed_tax: Self-employed (autónomos) must pay a monthly social security contribution (cuota) based on net income brackets, plus personal income tax (IRPF) paid via quarterly tax filings (Modelo 130).
ES.notes: Special tax regimes exist for residents in the Canary Islands (IGIC instead of VAT) and different regional deductions apply in Autonomous Communities like Madrid, Catalonia, and Andalusia.

IT.name: Italy
IT.vat: Standard rate is 22%, with reduced rates of 10%, 5%, and 4% for specific goods and services.
IT.income_tax: Progressive personal income tax (IRPEF) brackets range from 23% to 43%.
IT.self_employed_tax: Freelancers under the 'Regime Forfettario' pay a substitute tax of 15% (or 5% for the first five years) on a percentage of their revenue, plus mandatory INPS social security contributions.
IT.notes: Regional and municipal surcharges apply to the national income tax rate, varying by location.

IN.name: India
IN.vat: Goods and Services Tax (GST) is applied at rates of 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28% depending on the goods or services.
IN.income_tax: Progressive tax rates range from 0% up to 30% depending on income brackets under the old tax regime, with a concessional simplified regime also available.
IN.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals are taxed as per personal income tax slabs. Presumptive taxation schemes (Section 44ADA) exist for specified professionals allowing them to declare 50% of gross receipts as income to calculate tax.
IN.notes: Tax laws in India are subject to federal updates in the annual Union Budget. Non-residents may be subject to different tax treaty provisions.

BR.name: Brazil
BR.vat: Varies by state (ICMS) from 17% to 20%, plus federal IPI, PIS, and COFINS.
BR.income_tax: Progressive rates ranging from 0% (exempt) up to 27.5%.
BR.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals (Autônomos) pay income tax through the Carnê-Leão system at progressive rates, plus mandatory social security contributions (INSS) of 11% to 20% of their earnings.
BR.notes: Brazil's tax system is highly complex; many freelancers opt for the SIMPLES Nacional regime if they register as a micro-enterprise (ME) to reduce the total tax burden.

AR.name: Argentina
AR.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 5% to 35% on annual taxable income
AR.self_employed_tax: Monotributo system allows small taxpayers to pay a unified monthly fee covering income tax, VAT, and social security based on revenue brackets
AR.notes: The Monotributo system is widely used by freelancers and small businesses, but those exceeding certain revenue caps must transition to the General Regime

CL.name: Chile
CL.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 0% to 40% based on annual income thresholds.
CL.self_employed_tax: Independent workers are subject to a mandatory social security contribution system (pension, health, and labor accident insurance) and a 13.75% withholding tax rate as of 2024, which increases annually until reaching 17% in 2028.
CL.notes: The withholding tax rate for independent contractors is scheduled to increase incrementally each year until 2028 as part of tax reform.

CO.name: Colombia
CO.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 0% to 39% for residents on worldwide income.
CO.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals must pay mandatory social security contributions (health, pension, and occupational risk) and income tax based on annual earnings; those under the 'Simple Tax Regime' (Régimen SIMPLE) may benefit from unified lower tax rates.
CO.notes: Colombia offers the 'Régimen SIMPLE' which replaces corporate and income taxes with a single consolidated tax for eligible small businesses and freelancers, significantly simplifying tax compliance.

PE.name: Peru
PE.income_tax: Progressive rates ranging from 8% to 30% for residents.
PE.self_employed_tax: Classified under Fourth Category income; independent workers are subject to a withholding tax of 8% if annual income exceeds specific thresholds, or they may enroll in the Simplified Tax Regime (RUS).
PE.notes: Non-residents are subject to a flat 30% income tax rate on Peruvian-sourced income.

JP.name: Japan
JP.vat: 10% standard rate, with a reduced rate of 8% for food, beverages, and newspaper subscriptions.
JP.income_tax: Progressive tax rates from 5% to 45% based on taxable income tiers.
JP.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals pay income tax, national health insurance premiums, and a business tax if income exceeds 2.9 million JPY. They must file a tax return annually under the Blue or White return system.
JP.notes: Residents are also subject to a local inhabitant tax at a flat rate of 10% on previous year's income.

CN.name: People's Republic of China
CN.vat: Standard rate is 13%, with reduced rates of 9% and 6% for specific industries.
CN.income_tax: Progressive rates ranging from 3% to 45% for individuals; standard corporate income tax is 25%.
CN.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals and sole proprietors are generally subject to a progressive income tax rate of 5% to 35% on business income.
CN.notes: Tax policies can vary significantly between special economic zones and different provinces; individual tax residents are subject to global income tax.

AU.name: Australia
AU.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 0% to 45%, plus a 2% Medicare Levy for most residents.
AU.self_employed_tax: Sole traders pay income tax at the same progressive personal tax rates as employees; they are also responsible for the full Medicare Levy and must register for GST if annual turnover exceeds $75,000 AUD.
AU.notes: Self-employed individuals must pay their own superannuation contributions and may be subject to Pay As You Go (PAYG) installments.

KR.name: South Korea
KR.income_tax: Progressive rates ranging from 6% to 45% based on annual taxable income.
KR.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals pay global income tax on net profit and are required to contribute to the National Pension Service and National Health Insurance based on income and assets.
KR.notes: There is a standard local income tax surcharge of 10% on the national income tax liability.

SG.name: Singapore
SG.income_tax: Progressive resident rates from 0% to 24% for chargeable income above SGD 1,000,000
SG.self_employed_tax: Self-employed persons are taxed on net trade income at the same progressive resident rates as employees; mandatory contributions to the Central Provident Fund (CPF) are only required for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents on their Net Trade Income.
SG.notes: Non-residents are generally taxed at a flat rate of 15% or the progressive resident rates, whichever yields a higher tax amount, unless specific exemptions apply.

AF.name: Afghanistan
AF.vat: No formal VAT system implemented; Business Receipt Tax (BRT) applies at rates typically ranging from 2% to 10% on gross receipts.
AF.income_tax: Progressive tax rates for residents ranging from 0% to 20% on monthly income exceeding certain thresholds.
AF.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals are subject to the Business Receipt Tax (BRT) on gross turnover and personal income tax on net profits calculated after allowable deductions.
AF.notes: The tax environment remains highly volatile due to political instability and current administrative shifts. Compliance and enforcement are currently irregular.

AL.name: Albania
AL.vat: 20% standard rate, 6% reduced rate on hotels and tourism services, 0% on exports.
AL.income_tax: Progressive tax: 0% up to 30,000 ALL, 13% for 30,001–200,000 ALL, and 23% for amount exceeding 200,000 ALL per month.
AL.self_employed_tax: Small businesses with annual turnover up to 14,000,000 ALL pay 0% profit tax, while those exceeding this threshold pay 15% corporate tax. Professional services are subject to a 15% tax rate.
AL.notes: Self-employed individuals must also contribute to social and health insurance based on minimum and maximum thresholds set by the government annually.

DZ.name: Algeria
DZ.vat: Standard rate of 19%, with a reduced rate of 9% for certain essential goods and services.
DZ.income_tax: Progressive tax rates range from 0% to 35% on annual taxable income.
DZ.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals generally fall under the IFU (Impôt Forfaitaire Unique) system, a flat tax rate of 5% on annual turnover, provided revenue does not exceed 15 million DZD.
DZ.notes: Tax regulations in Algeria are subject to frequent updates through the annual Finance Act. Professional activities not eligible for IFU are subject to standard corporate or personal income tax reporting.

AD.name: Andorra
AD.income_tax: Progressive rates up to 10% on personal income.
AD.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals must pay a monthly social security contribution (CASS) which is typically 25% of the average national wage, and income tax on profits is capped at 10%.
AD.notes: Andorra offers a highly competitive tax regime with no tax on dividends from local companies and a simplified tax structure for professionals.

AO.name: Angola
AO.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals are generally subject to the Industrial Tax (Imposto Industrial) with a fixed rate of 25% or simplified schemes depending on the regime
AO.notes: Tax laws in Angola are subject to periodic changes; taxpayers should consult the AGT (Administração Geral Tributária) for specific regime classifications

AI.name: Anguilla
AI.self_employed_tax: 0% personal income tax on worldwide income
AI.notes: Anguilla is a tax-neutral jurisdiction with no income tax, capital gains tax, or VAT, though a 0.5% levy applies to certain financial transactions.

AQ.name: Antarctica
AQ.notes: Antarctica has no permanent residents, no government, and no taxation system. It is governed by the Antarctic Treaty System.

AG.name: Antigua and Barbuda
AG.income_tax: 0% on first 36,000 XCD; 25% on income exceeding 36,000 XCD.
AG.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals must register for the Antigua and Barbuda Social Security Scheme, with contributions typically split between employer/employee obligations, and are subject to the standard personal income tax rates.
AG.notes: Antigua and Barbuda has a simplified tax system where personal income tax was abolished for residents in 2016, but was subsequently reintroduced at a flat rate of 25% for income above a specific threshold.

AM.name: Armenia
AM.income_tax: Flat rate of 20% on personal income.
AM.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals (sole proprietors) can qualify for 'Micro-enterprise' status if annual turnover is below 24 million AMD, allowing for a 0% income tax rate, provided they are not in specific restricted industries.
AM.notes: Armenia offers various special tax regimes for IT and small businesses, including potential tax holidays or significantly reduced rates for certified startups.

AW.name: Aruba
AW.vat: Not applicable; Aruba uses a Turnover Tax (BBO) with a rate of 7%, plus supplemental levies (BAZV and BAVP) totaling 12.5% in some cases.
AW.income_tax: Progressive tax rates ranging from 7% to 52% depending on annual income brackets.
AW.self_employed_tax: Self-employed individuals are subject to the same progressive personal income tax rates as employees and are required to contribute to social security premiums including AOV/AWW and AZV.
AW.notes: Aruba is an autonomous country within the Kingdom of the Netherlands and operates under its own tax legislation distinct from the Netherlands.
